title: The Australian Stockman
content:
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The sun peers oן¿½er you wooded ridge and throן¿½ the forest dense,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Its golden edge oן¿½er the mountain ledge looks down on the stockyard fence,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Looks down, looks down, looks down on the stockyard fence;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And dark creeks rush throן¿½ the tangled brush, when their shuddering shadows throng
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Until they chime in the rude rough rhyme of the wild goburraן¿½s song.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  Chorus

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  Till they chime, ha! ha! till they chime, ha! ha! in the wild goburraן¿½s song;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  Till they chime, ha! ha! till they chime, ha! ha! in the wild goburraן¿½s song.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The night owl to her home hath fled, to shun the glorious pomp
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Of golden day she speeds away to her nest in the tea-tree swamp;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Away, away to her nest in the tea-tree swamp.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The dingo looks with a timid stare as he stealthily prowls along,
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ And his pattering feet in concert beat with the wild goburraן¿½s song.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  Chorus: And they beat, ha! ha! &c.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Oh! let them boast their cityן¿½s wealth, who toil in a dusty town;
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Give me the beam on the mountain stream, and the rangeן¿½s dark-faced frownן¿½
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ The stream, the stream, and the rangeן¿½s dark-faced frown.
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ When our steed shall pass oן¿½er the quivן¿½ring grass, and the crack of the sounding thong
ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ Shall bid the startled echoes join the wild goburraן¿½s song.

ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ ֲ  Chorus: And they join, ha! ha! &c.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton) represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton)'s bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
